extracted_evidence: The actual project period was longer than planned (ratio against the plan: 124%)

a delay in mobilizing the Special Assistance for Project Implementation by JICA and the interruptions of part of the construction works by the flooding in October 2010 – February 2011 and December 2012 – March 2013

extracted_evidence: the construction was completed in June 2012 (10 years and 10 months) (130 months). The project period was 260% longer than initially planned.

extracted_evidence: project outputs were implemented mostly as planned

extracted_evidence: Implementation was delayed because of (i) a change in the scope of works, i.e., from 4 lanes to 6 lanes; (ii) a change in the design of bridge piles due to seismic design provisions; and (iii) uncertain geotechnical conditions encountered during the piling works for one bridge.

response.commitment_rationale: The report explicitly states that implementation was delayed due to specific issues. While one of these is a scope change, the others are independent factors contributing to the delay. The presence of explicit delay language overrides any potential 'Normalcy Rule' inference.
response.label: Delay
